headline: Social Media Licensing Regime Covers 28 Platforms
context: MCMC mandatory licensing regime effective January 2026 requires platforms with over 8 million Malaysian users to obtain licences.

cards.big: 28 social media platforms must obtain MCMC licences from January 2026
cards.sub: Licensing requires local data storage content moderation and user reporting

cards.big: Platforms must appoint Malaysian-based representative for legal service
cards.sub: Non-compliance carries RM1 million daily penalty and potential blocking

cards.big: TikTok WhatsApp Facebook Instagram and Telegram among 28 affected
cards.sub: Each must submit quarterly content moderation reports to MCMC

cards.big: Digital rights groups warn licensing creates tool for censorship of dissent
cards.sub: Conditions include vague requirement to prevent content affecting public order

cards.big: Platform licensing tests whether regulation enables accountability or government control
28 platforms licensed — social media licensing works only if rules apply equally to all, including government.
